Details: At higher concentrations of MTBE, ozone was able to reduce up to 50% of MTBE, while at lower concentrations of MTBE, ozone reduced from 70 to 90% of MTBE. MTBE degradation occurred relatively slowly in conventional ozonation experiments due to the low reactivity of MTBE with molecular ozone. MTBE removal increased with increased ozone dose, but ozone doses were oftentimes higher than typically employed in drinking water treatment. MTBE oxidation by ozone may have to be optimized to minimize bromate formation.
